candidates.title: Auditing SAE Feature Absorption: Hedging Dominance, Rate-Distortion Diagnostics, and the L0 Phase Transition on JumpReLU SAEs
candidates.summary: First metric audit and confound decomposition of the Chanin absorption metric on JumpReLU SAEs (Gemma 2 2B). Three headline findings: (1) metric does not transfer -- shuffled controls exceed true labels in all 5 domains, 98.6% of detected FNs are hedging not competitive exclusion; (2) L0 phase transition from 42.9% to 0.8%, cross-layer stable; (3) CMI diagnostic separates absorbed/non-absorbed (d=-0.924) but does not survive Bonferroni (p=0.236). 8 iterations of evidence. Three blocking experiments (3 GPU-hrs) identified to advance from 6.5 to 8.0.
hypotheses: H1: Metric non-transfer to JumpReLU -- CONFIRMED across all 5 domains
H2: >80% hierarchy-driven at L0=22 -- FALSIFIED (1.4% hierarchy, 98.6% hedging)
H3: L0 phase transition in 40-80 range -- CONFIRMED (42.9% to 0.8%, CV<10%)
H4: CMI diagnostic -- PARTIALLY SUPPORTED (d=-0.924, p=0.236 Bonferroni)
H5: Cross-domain absorption >10% -- FALSIFIED (all below shuffled controls)
H8: Activation patching 7+/9 recovery -- PENDING (critical experiment)
H9: Strict hedging >80% of permissive -- PENDING
H10: CMI at L0=22 significant -- PENDING
candidates.pilot_focus: Three blocking experiments: activation patching on 9 persistent core words, tightened hedging classification, CMI replication at L0=22 with pre-registered d'=10
candidates.blocking_constraint: 3 GPU-hours of experiments unexecuted for 3 consecutive reviews

candidates.title: Geometric Forensics of Feature Absorption: Unsupervised Detection via Decoder Weight Topology in Sparse Autoencoders
candidates.summary: First unsupervised absorption detection method using four geometric signals from decoder weights: Gram matrix, activation-decoder misalignment, asymmetric co-activation deficit, and residual absorption score. Validated against supervised metric, then deployed on domains where supervised probes cannot be constructed. Fills Gap 7 (no probe-free absorption metric). Cross-domain connections to ecological competitive exclusion and neuroscience lateral inhibition.
hypotheses: Geometric AUROC >0.7 vs supervised absorption labels on first-letter task
Cross-reactive pairs (semantically unrelated but co-occurring) show geometric absorption signatures
Absorption landscape shows phase-transition behavior across SAE widths
candidates.pilot_focus: Compute decoder Gram matrix for one Gemma Scope 16k SAE, verify non-trivial cosine similarity distribution and co-activation deficits
candidates.blocking_constraint: requires validation against supervised ground truth

candidates.title: Immunological Imprinting in Sparse Autoencoders: Cross-Reactive Absorption and Frequency-Ratio Scaling Laws
candidates.summary: Original Antigenic Sin (OAS) analogy applied to SAE absorption. Predicts cross-reactive absorption (semantically unrelated but co-occurring features absorb each other), frequency-ratio scaling law, training-order imprinting, and masked regularization as conserved-epitope priming. The cross-reactive absorption prediction is unique and not made by any other perspective.
hypotheses: Cross-reactive absorption exists at rates predicted by frequency ratio, independent of hierarchy
P(absorption) scales with log(freq_i/freq_j) -- frequency-ratio scaling law
Features established early in training absorb more at matched final frequencies
Matryoshka reduces hierarchical but not cross-reactive absorption; OrtSAE reduces both
candidates.pilot_focus: Compute feature co-occurrence matrix, identify cross-reactive pairs (low decoder similarity, high co-occurrence), test extended absorption metric

candidates.title: Quantitative Theory of Feature Absorption: Coherence, Frequency, and the Absorption Phase Boundary
candidates.summary: First quantitative theory predicting absorption probability P(absorption) >= Phi(lambda * mu_H * sqrt(rho) / sigma - Phi^-1(1 - mu_H^2)) from decoder coherence mu_H, frequency ratio rho, and sparsity lambda. Derives critical width formula M_c >= K * (1 + D * mu_avg^2) / (1 - epsilon). Connects classical dictionary identifiability (Spielman et al., 2012) to SAE absorption. Formalizes absorption-hedging tradeoff.
hypotheses: Coherence-absorption correlation: Spearman rho >= 0.5 for max(mu_H) vs absorption rate
Same sigmoid model generalizes from spelling to city-country hierarchy
Critical width prediction matches empirical absorption vs width curves
candidates.pilot_focus: Compute decoder cosine similarity matrix, correlate with empirical absorption rates on first-letter task
candidates.blocking_constraint: mean-field approximation may not hold; Gaussian model may be poor fit

metadata.front_runner_rationale: cand_metric_audit has 8 iterations of accumulated evidence, all perspectives agree metric validation is the field's most urgent need, honest negative results (4/7 hypotheses falsified) are consistently the paper's strongest aspect, and the path to 8.0 is explicitly specified (3 GPU-hours). The stagnation at 6.5 is purely an execution problem (experiments not run), not a scientific problem.
metadata.elimination_rationale: No candidates eliminated. All three backups have genuine merit and serve different pivot scenarios: cand_geometry for follow-up paper, cand_immunological if cross-reactive absorption is discovered, cand_theory if quantitative predictions are validated.
